verses.latin_vulgate: Verba Ecclesiastæ, filii David, regis Jerusalem.
verses.king_james: The words of the Preacher, the son of David, king in Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vanitas vanitatum, dixit Ecclesiastes ;  vanitas vanitatum, et omnia vanitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲבֵ֤ל הֲבָלִים֙ אָמַ֣ר קֹהֶ֔לֶת הֲבֵ֥ל הֲבָלִ֖ים הַכֹּ֥ל הָֽבֶל׃
verses.king_james: Vanity of vanities, saith the Preacher, vanity of vanities; all is vanity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid habet amplius homo  de universo labore suo quo laborat sub sole ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־יִּתְר֖וֹן לָֽאָדָ֑ם בְּכׇ֨ל־עֲמָל֔וֹ שֶֽׁיַּעֲמֹ֖ל תַּ֥חַת הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: What profit hath a man of all his labour which he taketh under the sun?

verses.latin_vulgate: Generatio præterit, et generatio advenit ;  terra autem in æternum stat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דּ֤וֹר הֹלֵךְ֙ וְד֣וֹר בָּ֔א וְהָאָ֖רֶץ לְעוֹלָ֥ם עֹמָֽדֶת׃
verses.king_james: One generation passeth away, and another generation cometh: but the earth abideth for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oritur sol et occidit,  et ad locum suum revertitur ;  ibique renascens,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְזָרַ֥ח הַשֶּׁ֖מֶשׁ וּבָ֣א הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ וְאֶ֨ל־מְקוֹמ֔וֹ שׁוֹאֵ֛ף זוֹרֵ֥חַֽ ה֖וּא שָֽׁם׃
verses.king_james: The sun also ariseth, and the sun goeth down, and hasteth to his place where he arose.

verses.latin_vulgate: gyrat per meridiem, et flectitur ad aquilonem.  Lustrans universa in circuitu pergit spiritus,  et in circulos suos revertitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הוֹלֵךְ֙ אֶל־דָּר֔וֹם וְסוֹבֵ֖ב אֶל־צָפ֑וֹן סוֹבֵ֤ב ׀ סֹבֵב֙ הוֹלֵ֣ךְ הָר֔וּחַ וְעַל־סְבִיבֹתָ֖יו שָׁ֥ב הָרֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: The wind goeth toward the south, and turneth about unto the north; it whirleth about continually, and the wind returneth again according to his circuits.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia flumina intrant in mare,  et mare non redundat ;  ad locum unde exeunt flumina  revertuntur ut iterum fluant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־הַנְּחָלִים֙ הֹלְכִ֣ים אֶל־הַיָּ֔ם וְהַיָּ֖ם אֵינֶ֣נּוּ מָלֵ֑א אֶל־מְק֗וֹם שֶׁ֤הַנְּחָלִים֙ הֹֽלְכִ֔ים שָׁ֛ם הֵ֥ם שָׁבִ֖ים לָלָֽכֶת׃
verses.king_james: All the rivers run into the sea; yet the sea is not full; unto the place from whence the rivers come, thither they return again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cunctæ res difficiles ;  non potest eas homo explicare sermone.  Non saturatur oculus visu,  nec auris auditu impletur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־הַדְּבָרִ֣ים יְגֵעִ֔ים לֹא־יוּכַ֥ל אִ֖ישׁ לְדַבֵּ֑ר לֹא־תִשְׂבַּ֥ע עַ֙יִן֙ לִרְא֔וֹת וְלֹא־תִמָּלֵ֥א אֹ֖זֶן מִשְּׁמֹֽעַ׃
verses.king_james: All things are full of labour; man cannot utter it: the eye is not satisfied with seeing, nor the ear filled with hearing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid est quod fuit ? Ipsum quod futurum est.  Quid est quod factum est ? Ipsum quod faciendum est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־שֶּֽׁהָיָה֙ ה֣וּא שֶׁיִּהְיֶ֔ה וּמַה־שֶּׁנַּֽעֲשָׂ֔ה ה֖וּא שֶׁיֵּעָשֶׂ֑ה וְאֵ֥ין כׇּל־חָדָ֖שׁ תַּ֥חַת הַשָּֽׁמֶשׁ׃
verses.king_james: The thing that hath been, it is that which shall be; and that which is done is that which shall be done: and there is no new thing under the sun.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nihil sub sole novum,  nec valet quisquam dicere : Ecce hoc recens est :  jam enim præcessit in sæculis quæ fuerunt ante nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֵ֥שׁ דָּבָ֛ר שֶׁיֹּאמַ֥ר רְאֵה־זֶ֖ה חָדָ֣שׁ ה֑וּא כְּבָר֙ הָיָ֣ה לְעֹֽלָמִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר הָיָ֖ה מִלְּפָנֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Is there any thing whereof it may be said, See, this is new? it hath been already of old time, which was before us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non est priorum memoria ;  sed nec eorum quidem quæ postea futura sunt  erit recordatio apud eos qui futuri sunt in novissimo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֥ין זִכְר֖וֹן לָרִאשֹׁנִ֑ים וְגַ֨ם לָאַחֲרֹנִ֜ים שֶׁיִּהְי֗וּ לֹֽא־יִהְיֶ֤ה לָהֶם֙ זִכָּר֔וֹן עִ֥ם שֶׁיִּהְי֖וּ לָאַחֲרֹנָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: There is no remembrance of former things; neither shall there be any remembrance of things that are to come with those that shall come after.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego Ecclesiastes fui rex Israël in Jerusalem ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲנִ֣י קֹהֶ֗לֶת הָיִ֥יתִי מֶ֛לֶךְ עַל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל בִּירוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.king_james: I the Preacher was king over Israel in Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: et proposui in animo meo quærere et investigare sapienter  de omnibus quæ fiunt sub sole.  Hanc occupationem pessimam  dedit Deus filiis hominum, ut occuparentur in ea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנָתַ֣תִּי אֶת־לִבִּ֗י לִדְר֤וֹשׁ וְלָתוּר֙ בַּֽחׇכְמָ֔ה עַ֛ל כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר נַעֲשָׂ֖ה תַּ֣חַת הַשָּׁמָ֑יִם ה֣וּא ׀ עִנְיַ֣ן רָ֗ע נָתַ֧ן אֱלֹהִ֛ים לִבְנֵ֥י הָאָדָ֖ם לַעֲנ֥וֹת בּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: And I gave my heart to seek and search out by wisdom concerning all things that are done under heaven: this sore travail hath God given to the sons of man to be exercised therewith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidi cuncta quæ fiunt sub sole,  et ecce universa vanitas et afflictio spiritus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָאִ֙יתִי֙ אֶת־כׇּל־הַֽמַּעֲשִׂ֔ים שֶֽׁנַּעֲשׂ֖וּ תַּ֣חַת הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ וְהִנֵּ֥ה הַכֹּ֛ל הֶ֖בֶל וּרְע֥וּת רֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: I have seen all the works that are done under the sun; and, behold, all is vanity and vexation of spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Perversi difficile corriguntur,  et stultorum infinitus est numerus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְעֻוָּ֖ת לֹא־יוּכַ֣ל לִתְקֹ֑ן וְחֶסְר֖וֹן לֹא־יוּכַ֥ל לְהִמָּנֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: That which is crooked cannot be made straight: and that which is wanting cannot be numbered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Locutus sum in corde meo, dicens :  Ecce magnus effectus sum, et præcessi omnes sapientia  qui fuerunt ante me in Jerusalem ;  et mens mea contemplata est multa sapienter, et didici.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּבַּ֨רְתִּי אֲנִ֤י עִם־לִבִּי֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר אֲנִ֗י הִנֵּ֨ה הִגְדַּ֤לְתִּי וְהוֹסַ֙פְתִּי֙ חׇכְמָ֔ה עַ֛ל כׇּל־אֲשֶׁר־הָיָ֥ה לְפָנַ֖י עַל־יְרוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם וְלִבִּ֛י רָאָ֥ה הַרְבֵּ֖ה חׇכְמָ֥ה וָדָֽעַת׃
verses.king_james: I communed with mine own heart, saying, Lo, I am come to great estate, and have gotten more wisdom than all they that have been before me in Jerusalem: yea, my heart had great experience of wisdom and knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dedique cor meum ut scirem prudentiam atque doctrinam,  erroresque et stultitiam ;  et agnovi quod in his quoque esset labor et afflictio spiritus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאֶתְּנָ֤ה לִבִּי֙ לָדַ֣עַת חׇכְמָ֔ה וְדַ֥עַת הוֹלֵלֹ֖ת וְשִׂכְל֑וּת יָדַ֕עְתִּי שֶׁגַּם־זֶ֥ה ה֖וּא רַעְי֥וֹן רֽוּחַ׃
verses.king_james: And I gave my heart to know wisdom, and to know madness and folly: I perceived that this also is vexation of spirit.

verses.latin_vulgate: eo quod in multa sapientia multa sit indignatio ;  et qui addit scientiam, addit et laborem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֛י בְּרֹ֥ב חׇכְמָ֖ה רׇב־כָּ֑עַס וְיוֹסִ֥יף דַּ֖עַת יוֹסִ֥יף מַכְאֽוֹב׃
verses.king_james: For in much wisdom is much grief: and he that increaseth knowledge increaseth sorrow.
